2605.19718.title: CAIT: A Syntactic Parsing Toolkit for Child-Adult InTeractions
2605.19718.paper_summary: CHILDES is a paramount resource for language acquisition studies -- yet computational tools for analyzing its syntactic structure remain limited. Leveraging the recent release of the UD-English-CHILDES treebank with gold-standard Universal Dependencies (UD) annotations, we train a state-of-the-art dependency parser specifically tailored to CHILDES. The parser more accurately captures syntactic patterns in child--adult interactions, outperforming widely used off-the-shelf English parsers, including SpaCy and Stanza. Alongside the parser, we also release a Part-of-Speech tagger and an utterance-level construction tagger, which together form the open-source Syntactic Parsing Toolkit for Child--Adult InTeractions (CAIT). Through a detailed error analysis and a case study tracking the distribution of syntactic constructions across developmental time in CHILDES, we demonstrate the practical utility of the toolkit for large-scale, reproducible research on language acquisition.
2605.19718.paper_summary_zh: CHILDES 是語言習得研究的重要資源——然而，用於分析其句法結構的計算工具仍然有限。利用最近發布的 UD-English-CHILDES 樹庫，並附有金標準的通用依賴（UD）標註，我們訓練了一個專門為 CHILDES 設計的最先進依賴解析器。該解析器更準確地捕捉了兒童與成人互動中的句法模式，超越了廣泛使用的現成英語解析器，包括 SpaCy 和 Stanza。除了解析器，我們還發布了一個詞性標註器和一個語句級建構標註器，這兩者共同形成了開源的兒童與成人互動句法解析工具包（CAIT）。通過詳細的錯誤分析和一個案例研究，追蹤 CHILDES 中句法建構在發展時間上的分佈，我們展示了該工具包在大規模、可重複的語言習得研究中的實用性。
2605.19718.author: Francesca Padovani et.al.
2605.19718.authors: Francesca Padovani, Xiulin Yang, Bastian Bunzeck, Jaap Jumelet, Yevgen Matusevych, Nathan Schneider, Arianna Bisazza
